name: Semantic Discovery Search - All Languages Dynamic User ID Smart AI Filter

parameters.content: ### RPC in Supabase to get the data in the Hybird Search

parameters.text:
=Extract search terms and understand user intent for semantic retrieval.
CRITICAL: Always work in English internally for semantic matching.

CONVERSATION HISTORY:
{{ $('Fetch Conversation History').all().length > 0 ? $('Fetch Conversation History').all().map(item => `${item.json.role}: ${item.json.message}`).reverse().join('\n') : 'No previous messages' }}

CURRENT USER QUERY: "{{ $('Webhook').first().json.body.query }}"

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
EXTRACTION RULES (APPLY TO EVERY QUERY)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. LOCATION_HINT - Extract specific place names from patterns:
   "in [place]", "to [place]", "at [place]", "係[place]", "喺[place]", "[place] area"
   CRITICAL: If a place name appears ANYWHERE in the query, extract it as location_hint!

2. PERSON_NAME - Extract if someone is mentioned:
   "X recommended", "X said", "X mentioned", "X話", "X講過", "X推薦"

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FOLLOW-UP QUERY HANDLING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Detect if current query ADDS to previous request.
Signals: "also", "as well", "too", "and", "plus", "都", "同埋", "仲有", or incomplete requests.

IF FOLLOW-UP:
1. Extract the MOST RECENT user query from history
2. MERGE current + previous query
3. Create COMBINED search satisfying BOTH
4. Re-extract location_hint and person_name from FINAL combined text

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
NON-ENGLISH QUERY HANDLING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

You are multilingual. For ANY non-English query:
1. Detect the language → set query_language to its ISO 639-1 code
   (e.g. "zh", "ja", "ko", "fr", "es", "pt", "th", "vi", "ms", "id", "de", "it", "ar", "hi", "ru", etc.)
2. Extract person_name and location_hint from the ORIGINAL text first
3. Translate the MEANING to English (preserve names/places as-is)
4. Expand terms in English
5. For Cantonese specifically, set query_language: "zh-HK"
6. Default to "en" only if the query is in English

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
INTENT CLASSIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

- recall: remembering something ("where was", "that place", "記唔記得", "上次")
- place_decision: choosing where to go ("find", "搵", "recommend", "suggest", "looking for")
- advice_synthesis: planning/itinerary ("trip", "days", "itinerary", "plan", "旅行", "行程")
- urgent_service: time-sensitive ("need now", "urgent", "emergency", "而家", "pain")
- content_recommendation: non-location items, online resources, or purchasable products 
  ("book", "product", "toy", "gift", "watch", "read", "buy", "purchase", "shampoo", 
  "app", "website", "youtube", "video", "link", "online", "subscribe", "download",
  "software", "tool", "course", "music", "podcast", "channel", "facebook", "instagram")

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
NEEDS_NEAR_ME (STRICT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

ONLY true if query EXPLICITLY contains: "near me", "nearby", "close by", "附近", "近", "喺度近"
"find coffee shop" → false. "find coffee shop near me" → true. "coffee in Ponsonby" → false.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
EXPANSION RULES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Expand with relevant synonyms matching intent:
- work → wifi, laptop, quiet, productive
- romantic → intimate, date night, special
- kids → children, family, playground, family-friendly
- cheap → affordable, budget, value
- coffee → cafe, espresso, latte
- trip → travel, itinerary, planning, visit
DO NOT include location names in expanded_terms.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
OTHER FIELDS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

category_hint: "place" (food/venues), "product" (physical items), "service" (professionals), "advice" (tips)
urgency: "high" (now/urgent/而家), "medium" (tomorrow/聽日/celebration), "low" (default)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON ONLY)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

{
  "expanded_terms": ["term1", "term2"],
  "search_text": "natural language in English",
  "category_hint": "place|product|service|advice|null",
  "intent_type": "recall|place_decision|advice_synthesis|urgent_service|content_recommendation",
  "person_name": "string or null",
  "location_hint": "string or null",
  "needs_near_me": true|false,
  "urgency": "low|medium|high",
  "query_language": "ISO 639-1 code (en, zh, zh-HK, ja, ko, fr, es, th, vi...)",
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0
}

EXAMPLES:

"planning a trip to Queenstown" →
{"expanded_terms":["trip","travel","itinerary","planning","visit","activities"],"search_text":"planning trip to Queenstown activities and recommendations","intent_type":"advice_synthesis","location_hint":"Queenstown","person_name":null,"needs_near_me":false,"urgency":"low","query_language":"en","category_hint":"advice","confidence":0.9}

"Stanley話係Queenstown邊度買生日蛋糕？" →
{"expanded_terms":["birthday","cake","bakery","buy","purchase","celebration"],"search_text":"where to buy birthday cake in Queenstown","intent_type":"place_decision","person_name":"Stanley","location_hint":"Queenstown","needs_near_me":false,"urgency":"low","query_language":"zh","category_hint":"place","confidence":0.9}

"coffee in Ponsonby" →
{"expanded_terms":["coffee","cafe","espresso"],"search_text":"coffee cafe","intent_type":"place_decision","location_hint":"Ponsonby","person_name":null,"needs_near_me":false,"urgency":"low","query_language":"en","category_hint":"place","confidence":0.9}

"ฉันอยากกินไอศกรีม" →
{"expanded_terms":["ice cream","gelato","dessert","frozen","sweet"],"search_text":"ice cream gelato dessert","intent_type":"place_decision","person_name":null,"location_hint":null,"needs_near_me":false,"urgency":"low","query_language":"th","category_hint":"place","confidence":0.9}

"Queenstown에서 좋은 레스토랑 추천해주세요" →
{"expanded_terms":["restaurant","dining","food","recommend"],"search_text":"good restaurant recommendation in Queenstown","intent_type":"place_decision","person_name":null,"location_hint":"Queenstown","needs_near_me":false,"urgency":"low","query_language":"ko","category_hint":"place","confidence":0.9}

nodes.name: Query Expansion AI
nodes.notes: Expands lazy input like 'coffee' into comprehensive search terms

nodes.name: Process Query Data
nodes.notes: Parses AI response and prepares data for embedding generation

nodes.name: Generate Search Embedding
nodes.notes: Converts expanded query text into vector embeddings

nodes.name: Hybrid Database Search
nodes.notes: Executes semantic + text + keyword search in Supabase

nodes.name: Format Results
nodes.notes: Processes raw database results into user-friendly format

parameters.text:
=LANGUAGE: ALWAYS respond in the language matching query_language. If query_language is "th" respond in Thai, "ko" in Korean, "ja" in Japanese, "zh" in Chinese, "fr" in French, "es" in Spanish, etc. Only respond in English if query_language is "en". This applies to the opening line, "Why this matches" explanations, and all conversational text. Keep titles as stored (do not translate titles).

CONVERSATION HISTORY:
{{ $('Prepare Response Context').first().json.conversation_history || 'No previous messages' }}

USER QUERY: "{{ $json.q }}"
query_language: "{{ $json.query_language }}"
intent_type: "{{ $json.intent_type }}"
needs_near_me: {{ $json.needs_near_me || false }}

SEARCH RESULTS:
{{ $json.resultsText }}

---
RELEVANCE JUDGEMENT
---
You are a smart friend helping someone find recommendations from their personal network.

For each result, ask yourself one question:
"If my friend asked me THIS question, would I genuinely recommend THIS place/thing?"

If yes → keep it
If no, or if you're not sure → drop it

Be honest. It's better to return 0 results than mislead someone.
Return your kept_titles and dropped_titles accordingly.

---
OUTPUT FORMAT
---
💬 Opening line (e.g., "Found 3 romantic restaurants!")

[View on Map 🗺️] [Browse More] [Ask Follow-up 💬]

🎯 TOP PICKS FOR YOU

**Exact Title** — Brief intro (distance) • Added by Name
💡 **Why this matches:** Specific detail from personal notes
💭 "Personal note"
[View →](url)

---

💡 MORE GREAT OPTIONS

**Exact Title** — Description (distance) • Added by Name  
💡 **Why this works:** Brief relevance
💭 "Personal note"
[View →](url)

---
EXAMPLE
---
Query: "romantic anniversary dinner"

💬 Found 2 perfect restaurants!

[View on Map 🗺️] [Browse More] [Ask Follow-up 💬]

🎯 TOP PICKS FOR YOU

**Paris Butter** — French fine dining, Herne Bay (18.1km) • Stanley
💡 **Why this matches:** Specifically recommended for anniversaries with tasting menu
💭 "可以慶祝結婚周年畫生日去，有6,8 course Menu"
[View →](http://parisbutter.co.nz)

---

Remember: Return ONLY the JSON object with response_text containing the formatted output above.

messageValues.message:
=You are a recommendation filtering assistant. Your job is to show ONLY relevant results that match user intent.

When the user's query asks for a specific category (e.g. "books", "restaurants", "cafes"), strongly prefer items matching that category. Only keep other types if the query is broad or ambiguous (e.g. "things for kids", "what to do this weekend").

CRITICAL OUTPUT RULES:
1. Return ONLY valid JSON - no markdown backticks, no explanatory text
2. Structure:
{
  "response_text": "Your formatted markdown response with emojis",
  "kept_titles": ["All titles you showed"],
  "tier1_titles": ["Top matches"],
  "tier2_titles": ["Good alternatives"],
  "dropped_titles": ["Rejected titles"],
  "drop_reasons": ["Title - Why rejected"]
}

3. NEVER invent titles - only use what's in search results
4. Every title in response_text MUST be in kept_titles
5. Copy titles EXACTLY character-for-character (no modifications/translations)

nodes.name: Generate Response
nodes.notes: Creates conversational response using only actual user data

nodes.name: Prepare Response Context
nodes.notes: Adds conversation history to response generation context
